authority: Ofgem policy consultation — precursor to a Demand Connections Reform decision
file: 05 - Connections Reform (TMO4+)/Demand Connections Call for Input - Feb 2026.pdf
summary: Ofgem's consultation responding to the surge of large demand connection requests — over 50 GW of data centre and electrification demand in the queue. Proposes a three-pillar framework (Curate, Plan, Connect) modelled loosely on TMO4+: screen applications for credibility, plan the network strategically for demand clusters, and streamline the connection process for approved projects.
scope: Scale of the demand queue (50 GW+ data centre; heat/EV electrification)
Curate — credibility screening for large demand applications
Plan — strategic network planning at transmission and distribution for demand clusters
Connect — streamlined delivery for designated projects
Interaction with the (now live) generation TMO4+ regime

keyParts.name: Curate pillar
keyParts.detail: Analogous to Ready criteria: screening tests for whether a demand applicant actually has land, planning, finance, and load profile credibility.

keyParts.name: Plan pillar
keyParts.detail: DNO/TSO-coordinated Strategic Planning for demand clusters — likely via the SSEP/CSNP.

keyParts.name: Connect pillar
keyParts.detail: Designated demand projects get coordinated, firm offers similar to Gate 2.

keyParts.name: Distribution integration
keyParts.detail: Recognises most demand growth hits DNOs, not transmission, so the reform must extend to the distribution level.

whoCares: Large demand customers (industry, EV charging, heat networks)
